title: asplenium subglandulosum subsp. papaverifolium
intro: Asplenium subglandulosum subsp. papaverifolium, also known as the Papaver-leaved spleenwort, is a subspecies of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Asplenium subglandulosum subsp. papaverifolium include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
